metrics.top_terms: canary, paper, this, that, markdown, from, claim, files, does, codex, claims, working, grader, should, produce

sections.title: Codex Canary Paper
sections.text:
This paper claims that a working paper grader should produce durable JSON,
Markdown, HTML, CSV, and Excel outputs from a small markdown source.

sections.title: The claim is testable because the pipeline either emits those files or it does
sections.text:
not. If no output files are produced, the workflow fails the canary.
The model does not prove theological truth from this canary. It only verifies
that the intake, claim audit, report export, and archive lanes are functioning.

claims.section: The claim is testable because the pipeline either emits those files or it does
claims.one_sentence_claim: The model does not prove theological truth from this canary.
claims.forward_test: If the claim is true, the described pattern should appear where the paper says it should appear.
claims.reverse_test: If the pattern can appear without the proposed framework, the claim should be downgraded or narrowed.
claims.evidence_bar: No explicit evidence marker in sentence.
claims.kill_conditions: Needs an explicit failure case: what observation, logic result, or counterexample would make this claim false?
claims.not_claimed: Does not by itself claim that physics proves theology.
claims.proof_boundary: Current boundary: deterministic pass classifies this as Formal Model, not a final proof.
